title: Breitmann in Paris
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Recessit in Franciam.ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Et affectu pectoris,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Et toto gestu corporis,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Et scholares maxime,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Qui festa colunt optime.ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ - Carmina Burana, 13th century.



ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der teufelן¿½s los in Bal Mabille,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dereן¿½s hell-fire in de air,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De fiddlers canן¿½t blay noding else
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Boot Orphן¿½e aux Enfers:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vot makes de beoples howl mit shoy?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Da capo Bravo! bis!!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Itן¿½s a Deutscher aus Amerikן¿½:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Hans Breitmann in Paris.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dereן¿½s silber toughts vot might hafe peen,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dereן¿½s golden deeds vot must:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der Hans ish come to Frankenland
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ On one eternal bust.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der same old rowdy Argonaut
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vot hoont de same oldt vleece,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A hafin all de foon dere ish
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der Breitmann in Paris.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mit a gal on eider shoulder
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A holdin py his beard,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He tantz de Cancan, sacrament!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dill all das Volk vas skeered.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Like a roarin hippopatamos,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mit a kangarunic shoomp,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey feared heן¿½d smash de Catacombs,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Each dime der Breitmann bump.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De pretty liddle cocodettes
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Lofe efery dings ish new,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Dן¿½ou vient il donc ce grand Mן¿½sieu?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ O sacrן¿½ nom de Dieu!ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In fain dey kicks deir veet on high,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And sky like vlyin geese,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey can not kick de hat afay
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ From Breitmann in Paris.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ O vhere vas id der Breitmann life?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Oopon de Rond Point gay,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vot shdreet lie shoost pehind his house?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ La rue de Rabelais.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Aroundt de corner Harperן¿½s shtands
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhere Yankee drinks dey mill,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhile shdraight ahet, agross de shdreet,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der lies de Bal Mabille.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Idן¿½s all along de Elysן¿½es,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Idן¿½s oop de Boulevarce,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Heן¿½s sampled all de weinshops,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und heן¿½s vinked at efery garce.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dou schveet plack-silken Gabrielle,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ O let me learn from dee,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ If ן¿½tis in lofe or absinthe drunks,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dat dis wild ghost may pe?

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und dou mayן¿½st kneel in Notre Dame,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und veep away dy sin,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhile I go vight at Barriere balls,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Oontil mine poots cave in;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Boot if ve pray, or if ve sin
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhile nodings ish refuse,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Tis all de same in Paris here,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ So long ash lן¿½on sן¿½amuse.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ O life, mein dear, at pest or vorst,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ish boot a vancy ball,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Its cratest shoy a vild gallop,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhere madness goferns all.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und should dey toorn ids gas-light off,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und nefer leafe a shbark,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Sdill Iן¿½d find my vay to Heafen or
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dy lips, lofe, in de dark.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ O crown your het mit roses, lofe!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ O keep a liddel sprung!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Oonendless wisdom ish but dis:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To go it vhile youן¿½re yung!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und Age vas nefer coom to him,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To him Spring plooms afresh,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Who finds a livinן¿½ spirit in
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der Teufel und der Flesh.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Charles G. Leland represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Charles G. Leland's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
